title: Emergency Wildfire Fighting Technology Act of 2025
short_title: Emergency Wildfire Fighting Technology Act of 2025
plain_summary: This new law makes the Forest Service and Department of the Interior study a special firefighting system called CAFFS. This system uses throwaway containers that can be dropped from airplanes to carry water or chemicals that help put out fires. The goal is to have more planes available to fight wildfires during emergencies. The government agencies must finish studying this technology within 90 days and then write a report for Congress within 120 days about whether it works well.
last_action_text: Received in the Senate and Read twice and referred to the Committee on Agriculture, Nutrition, and Forestry.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Emergency Wildfire Fighting Technology Act of two thousand twenty five.

sections.title: Section 2 Container Aerial Firefighting System (CAFFS)
sections.text: The Secretary of Agriculture and the Secretary of the Interior must work together to study a new firefighting technology. This technology is called the container aerial firefighting system or CAFFS. They must finish this study within ninety days after this law is passed. Two expert groups will help them with this study. These groups are the National Interagency Aviation Committee and the Interagency Airtanker Board. The study will look at how well CAFFS works to fight and stop wildfires. After the study is done, both Secretaries must write a report about what they found. They have one hundred twenty days after this law is passed to give this report to Congress. The same expert groups will help them write the report. The report goes to specific committees in the House of Representatives and the Senate. In the House, it goes to the Agriculture Committee and the Natural Resources Committee. In the Senate, it goes to the Agriculture, Nutrition, and Forestry Committee and the Energy and Natural Resources Committee.
